subject: Physics
title: Rutherford Discovers the Nucleus
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Rutherford Discovers the Nucleus.

hook.text: In 1911, Ernest Rutherford fired particles at gold foil and discovered atoms are mostly empty space.

buildup.text: He expected alpha particles to pass straight through the thin gold sheet. Most did.

discovery.text: But some particles bounced straight back—as if hitting something incredibly dense and tiny inside the atom.

twist.text: Rutherford said it was like firing a cannon at tissue paper and having the shell come back at you.

climax.text: He proved atoms have a tiny, dense nucleus at the center with electrons orbiting in mostly empty space.

punchline.text: Gold foil and a surprise bounce. The atomic nucleus revealed.

quiz.question: What did Rutherford discover from the gold foil experiment?
options: Atoms have a tiny dense nucleus surrounded by empty space
Electrons are found inside the nucleus
